title: Gomesa gilva
intro: Gomesa gilva, also known as the yellow-striped Gomesa, is a species of orchid that is native to South America, particularly in the tropical regions of Brazil and Peru. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Gomesa gilva include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and extracts, which are often used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including anxiety and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Gomesa gilva is classified within the Orchidaceae family. This family is further divided into various subfamilies, with Gomesa gilva being part of the Epidendroideae subfamily. It belongs to the Oncidiinae tribe within the Epidendroideae subfamily. Gomesa gilva is also commonly classified as part of the Oncidium genus, although some sources may group it under a different genus due to ongoing taxonomic revisions. Further research may be necessary to confirm the most up-to-date classification of Gomesa gilva.
